ERROR: Status effect Behemoth Pregnancy Ends” does not exist!
\n\n
Next
It’s time. The baby is coming. You don’t know how you know it, you just do, and with a degree of certainty you’ve rarely ever felt in your life.
\n\nWith a strange, almost eerie calmness, you

go to a medical facility and inform them of your condition. The staff doesn’t need a second look at your gargantuan belly to understand the gravity of the situation – they prepare a room and perform all necessary exams in record time. You are advised to have a C-section, but, for some reason, you just know you don’t need it. The doctor only gives in after you mention your microsurgeons and your status as a Steele, but they still have you sign papers that exempt them from all responsibility, should things go south. Oh well.

set up a hardlight tent and prepare everything you deem necessary: sheets, a soft pillow, warm water, several towels, cotton, disinfectant... "+(pc.hasGooArmor()?"[gooarmor.name] assumes her humanoid form and stays ready to assist you to the best of her medical capabilities.":"")

\n\nAs the time between your cramps gets shorter, you use breathing techniques to reduce the discomfort, though it turns out you’re feeling far less pain than you "+(n?"and the staff":"")+" expected. Soon enough, your water breaks, and the baby comes less than an hour after.[pc.hasLegs| Spreading your legs wide| Getting in position], you start to push "+(n?"as the doctor instructs.":"with the specific technique that you learned when you were preparing for this.")
[gooarmor.name] holds your hand the entire time, cooing both encouragements and instructions.

\n\nSweat glistens on your brow from the sheer effort of giving birth to a half-behemoth, yet it doesn’t hurt nearly as much as it should. You watch in a mix of awe and disbelief as the baby’s head starts to emerge – it shouldn’t be possible for a person your size to birth a creature this large! The only explanation you can think of is that your microsurgeons worked with behemoth genes to give you the incredible stretchiness that their women have.
The staff looks as baffled as you, almost forgetting their job for a moment, but a stern look is enough to remind them.
Another few minutes, and the rest of the baby comes out, then
the doctor waits
[gooarmor.name] waits
you wait
a minute to cut the umbilical cord and finish the procedures.

\n\nHowever, there is little time to rest, as a second baby starts coming out just a few minutes after the first!"+(n?" Turns out it was ‘hidden’ behind the first child and didn’t show in the hasty scans performed by the staff.":"")+" Some time later...
With professional efficiency, the staff cleans up the "+(a?"babies":"baby")+" and brings them to you, so that you can
[gooarmor.name] quickly cleans up the "+(a?"babies":"baby")+" and brings them to you, so that you can
You gently clean up the "+(a?"babies":"baby")+" and

hold them. Oof, so heavy! As soon as you take the "+(a?"children":"child")+" in your arms, they open their vibrant eyes, and you immediately feel a psychic connection. They know you’re their[pc.mfn| parent| mother| parent], and they love you as much as you love them. The feeling makes your chest warm, and a tender smile comes to your lips unbidden.
\n\n"+(n?"The doctor has trouble telling":"It’s hard to tell")+" the "+(a?"babies’":"baby’s")+" sex by looks alone, since a genital slit could be easily mistaken for a vagina, but you’re pretty sure
the twins are
boys.
girls.
herms, though there’s no telling in which direction they’ll lean as they grow older.
it’s a
boy.
girl.
herm, though there’s no telling in which direction they’ll lean as they grow older.

\n\nThe "+(a?"childrens’":"child’s")+" face seems to be human in structure, taking after[pc.ora human| you| the human side of your genes], but they have little holes for ears and a thick, tapered tail. Moreover, you can make out the hardness of semi-transparent scales on their forelimbs, and their spine is lined with little bumps that you suppose will become psychoactive crystals as they grow up.

\n\nYou take the chance to "+(pc.isLactating()?"breastfeed the gluttonous little":"admire your little")+" kaiju for a few minutes and then put them to sleep with a lullaby."+(pc.isLactating()?" Thankfully, they know to retract their sharp little teeth when drinking from your chest.":"")+" Once everything is said and done, exhaustion sets in."+(n?"":" Before passing out yourself, you gently tuck them in a pod and send them on their first trip through space, all the way to Tavros’ nursery...")+" Now. You just need. Some sleep...

ERROR: Status effect Dzaan Pregnancy Ends” does not exist!
\n\n
Next
Pain in your gut bends you over and fluid spills
onto the deck
onto the ground
into your [pc.lowerUndergarment]
. This is it: your child is making its way down your birth canal.
\n\nYou grab the nearest medkit and head for your bed, determined to deliver the baby safely. After setting aside your gear, you lie down and begin to breathe in preparation for your labor.

\n\nGroaning at the timing, you shed your [pc.gear] and position yourself the best you can in the inhospitable and non-hospital-able terrain. The wish that you’d stayed somewhere indoors and safe hums through your thoughts like a mosquito, but there’s no help for it - you’ll have to deliver the baby on your own.

\n\nYou waddle as fast as you can to the nearest restroom. There’s a med-drone fastened to the wall, installed for this exact eventuality. It instructs you to remain calm as it positions a blanket beneath you, and to bear down when it beeps.
\n\nSpasms wrack your pregnant body for the next hour as it works the baby free. It is a tough, arduous process: Your bundle of joy is a big so-and-so, struggling and hard-boned.
You have no choice but to bear it; richly cursing the baby, yourself, and the world at large for having to bear a child in this day and age like this.
(o?"The drone administers":"You administer")+" some painkillers, and the whole thing goes on in a fuzzy blur.

\n\nAt last you hear a throaty wail; somebody announcing themselves to the universe. Exhausted, you cut the umbilical cord and draw the damp, wriggling child up into your arms. To the casual observer perhaps, it would look like you’ve given birth to a brown-skinned, particularly large human girl. A mother’s eye lands upon the long, preternaturally curved back; the pointed posture of the feet, and the keratinous heels that support them; and of course, the pointed, elven ears.

\n\nYour dzaan child is "+(0===t?"female, a so-called beta of the species":"a hermaphrodite, a rare alpha of the species")+", and once she’s stopped contorting her face into hearty screams and is contenting herself with the [pc.milk]-dripping [pc.nipple] you present to her, you can see for yourself that she is both whole and a real cutie.

\n\nYou nurse her peacefully for a while, lost in the throes of exhausted motherhood. At last her head droops against your arm, carried off into a deep sleep. You summon the drone, carefully sequester her into the climate-controlled device, and watch it bear your new child off to the nursery, where years of quiet safety await. You clean yourself up, and then disappear into a long, well-needed slumber yourself.
